organizations.role: Board Member; National Jewish Democratic Council secretary
organizations.notes: CEO Go! Retail/Calendar Club; documented ADL Austin involvement; also formerly secretary Elie Wiesel Foundation for Humanity

organizations.role: True Colors: Shake the Hate Featured Speaker (2012)
organizations.notes: Featured speaker. Muslim woman who shared story about facing discrimination post-9/11 in Austin. Introduced by ADL Austin Community Director Karen Gross.

organizations.role: Board involvement (Adler-era); 2017 Maislin Humanitarian Award co-honoree
organizations.notes: Spouse of former Mayor Steve Adler; DT Land Group commercial real estate

organizations.role: Past Board Chair (2009-2012); helped create Austin Hate Crimes Task Force; expanded No Place for Hate program
organizations.notes: Wikipedia biography of Steve Adler (58th Austin mayor 2015-2023) explicitly documents he "served as the board chair of the Anti-Defamation League Austin Region" from 2009 to 2012 and notes ADL board-chair work alongside Texas Tribune/Ballet Austin civic roles. Disambiguation: Fuentes donor listed as "mayor" at Austin 78701 matches Steve Adler, former Austin mayor.
Helped establish the Austin ADL chapter. Per PolitiFact 2014: led board 2009-2012. Per Forward: "He made ADL the go-to civil rights organization in Austin" — Kirk Rudy quote.
Multiple independent sources confirm ADL board role. Also on Science Mill board, NWACA, etc.
CURRENT ADL board member (10+ years). Armbrust and Brown attorney (Austin commercial real estate law firm). Land use / business litigation practice.

Texas Book Festival board bio explicitly identifies Marc Winkelman as "secretary of the National Jewish Democratic Council". NJDC is a Democratic-aligned Jewish pro-Israel political organization. Disambiguation: Fuentes Winkelman, Marc / calendar executive services / Austin 78744 = Marc Winkelman, CEO Calendar Club / Calendar Holdings (Austin HQ).
Paradigm = software/analytics for global oil & gas E&P (subsurface modeling, well planning, drilling). Now CEO of Avetta (supply chain SaaS, not O&G). Historical oil career connection.

organizations.org: Austin Women in Oil and Gas
organizations.role: Administrative Chair and Board of Directors (2019-present)
organizations.notes: Partner at Holland & Knight (formerly Thompson & Knight); practice covers oil & gas regulatory and transactional matters in Eagle Ford, Bakken, Permian Basin

organizations.org: Kay Bailey Hutchison Center for Energy, Law & Business (UT Austin)
organizations.notes: Energy law and business advisory council at UT Austin
Shell supermajor. Houston TX 77077 zip matches Shell corridor.

organizations.role: 27-year partner at V&E (nations leading energy law firm); headed ADR section
organizations.notes: First woman to make partner while having children at V&E. 2006 TX US Senate candidate (D). Nearly 3 decades embedded in top energy law firm.

organizations.role: 39 years O&G: 24yr ExxonMobil + 15yr Schlumberger Lead Reservoir Engineer; now Principal Consultant
organizations.notes: PhD Petroleum Engineering Stanford 1977. Co-author SPE Perforating monograph. 30+ SPE papers. Listed Not Employed but active consultant.

organizations.role: Founder; AAPG Certified Petroleum Geologist; 30+ years upstream O&G
organizations.notes: Retired geologist. Former Manager of Exploration for NYSE-listed O&G company. Stafford TX.

organizations.role: Deputy GC at Workrise (O&G workforce platform); formerly Sr Associate at V&E (top energy law firm)
organizations.notes: Career arc runs through O&G: V&E (nations top energy law firm) then in-house at Workrise (25K+ O&G workers platform)
